alerts.title: Invisible peacebuilders: How ‘diplomacy works’ to prevent conflict
alerts.summary: Described as “sometimes modest, sometimes historic,” UN special political missions have quietly been preventing the escalation of conflicts during the organization’s 80-year history, becoming a key instrument for maintaining peace.

alerts.title: Civilian dangers multiply as drones transform Ukraine's battlefield
alerts.summary: As drones reshape the battlefield in Ukraine, they are also creating new and increasingly complex dangers for civilians, threatening recovery efforts, agriculture and global food security long after the fighting ends.

alerts.title: Sudan’s people subject to ‘relentless’ drone strikes in El Obeid
alerts.summary: The war in Sudan threatens to spiral further as the strategically-important town of El Obeid continues to face “relentless” drone attacks by advancing paramilitary militias, the UN’s human rights chief, Volker Türk, said on Friday.

alerts.title: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Iranian attack in Bahrain
alerts.summary: The 15-member Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Thursday morning to address the escalating tensions in the Gulf region following Iran attacks in recent days on Bahrain and Kuwait while Tehran and Washington continue to trade strikes over the Strait of Hormuz chokepoint. Stay tuned for live updates.

alerts.title: Ukraine: Rescuers work to save trapped residents after wave of Russian attacks
alerts.summary: Aid agencies in Ukraine on Thursday rushed to respond to a massive uptick in deadly strikes by Russia, including one of the largest attacks on Kyiv to date.

alerts.title: Expanding areas under Israeli control in Gaza increase risks to civilians, UN warns
alerts.summary: The continued expansion of areas under Israeli control in Gaza since October’s ceasefire agreement is placing civilians at greater risk and severely constraining humanitarian operations, the UN and humanitarian partners&nbsp;warned on Wednesday.

alerts.title: World News in Brief: Risk of atrocities in Sudan, UN programme prevents climate displacement, more civilians killed in Ukraine
alerts.summary: Drone attacks by advancing rebel fighters who are besieging El Obeid,&nbsp;Sudan, continue to result in civilian casualties, putting around half a million civilians at grave risk of large-scale atrocities.

alerts.title: Despite record $100 million shortfall, Palestine relief agency still ‘a critical platform’ for Gaza recovery
alerts.summary: The UN agency serving 5.9 million Palestine refugees, UNRWA, continues to strive to deliver on its mandate while facing an unprecedented $100 million budget shortfall, a gap it hopes to narrow during Tuesday’s pledging conference at UN Headquarters.

alerts.title: Strait of Hormuz: Gradual re-opening is no quick fix for developing nations, UN warns
alerts.summary: Re-opening the Strait of Hormuz would bring vital relief for many economies, but developing countries will continue to grapple with increased food and fuel costs, according to&nbsp;a new UN report released on Tuesday.

alerts.title: Middle East conflict leaves developing countries paying the price
alerts.summary: With another weekend of strikes involving the United States and Iran, renewed diplomatic efforts on Monday have eased immediate fears of escalation, but the economic shockwaves are already taking a heavy toll on some of the world's poorest countries.

alerts.title: Security Council LIVE: Spotlight on Israeli settlements in occupied Palestinian territory
alerts.summary: Israeli settlement activities in occupied Palestinian territory, declared illegal under a decade-old resolution, are under the spotlight at the Security Council’s open meeting at 10am local time on Monday, with updates from the office of the UN Special Coordinator for the Middle East Peace Process and two civil society representatives. Stay tuned for live updates.

alerts.title: World News in Brief: Afghanistan airstrikes, rights violations in DR Congo, Gaza displacement
alerts.summary: At least 28 civilians were killed and 49 injured in airstrikes carried out in Paktya, Paktika and Kunar provinces in Afghanistan on Sunday evening, the UN Mission in the country has reported.

alerts.title: International cooperation critical as terrorism threat evolves
alerts.summary: Terrorism is evolving amid global instability characterised by heightened geopolitical tensions, protracted conflicts and growing fragmentation – and the international response must also evolve to meet the challenge.&nbsp;

alerts.title: UN details humanitarian toll of strikes on Ukrainian power industry
alerts.summary: Missile and drone attacks killed at least a dozen civilians in Russia and Ukraine over the weekend as both countries continue to launch long-range drone strikes.

alerts.title: Strait of Hormuz: UN evacuates 2,500 seafarers before attack freezes rescue operation
alerts.summary: The United Nations’ maritime agency said on Friday that it had successfully evacuated about 2,500 stranded seafarers from the Persian Gulf before suspending the operation, after an attack on a commercial vessel exposed uncertainty over who can guarantee safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

alerts.title: UN envoy urges parties to ‘stay the course’ towards peace in eastern DR Congo
alerts.summary: The new head of the UN mission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has urged parties to the peace processes for the volatile eastern region to maintain momentum and fully implement their commitments.

alerts.title: Sudan: Window is closing to prevent wider escalation in El Obeid
alerts.summary: A senior United Nations official warned on Friday that the opportunity to prevent a major escalation in the Sudanese city of El Obeid is “rapidly narrowing” as fighting intensifies in and around the North Kordofan state capital.

alerts.title: Security Council extends critical stabilisation force in Syria
alerts.summary: The 15-member Security Council unanimously adopted a draft resolution on Thursday renewing the mandate of the UN Disengagement Observer Force (UNDOF) for six months.

alerts.title: From Lebanon to the Strait of Hormuz, a Middle East hanging on fragile peace talks
alerts.summary: Since Tuesday, United Nations peacekeepers have detected no airstrikes or new missile fire in southern Lebanon. The lull remains fragile, however, as Israeli drones continue to fly over the area and military operations continue on the ground.

alerts.title: UN envoy warns of growing risks in El Obeid as Sudan conflict drags on
alerts.summary: The United Nations Secretary-General’s Personal Envoy for Sudan voiced alarm on Wednesday over escalating violence in and around the city of El Obeid, warning that further military escalation could put thousands of civilians at risk and deepen Sudan’s already devastating humanitarian crisis.

alerts.title: LIVE: From conflict to stability, how the UN is supporting peacebuilding around the world
alerts.summary: As wars persist in different parts of the world, it’s perhaps easy to lose sight of the many countries that have managed to recover from past violence, sustain peace and prevent conflict. The UN Peacebuilding Fund, which is marking its 20th year of operation in 2026, has contributed to many of these success stories. UN News app users click here.

alerts.title: Thousands trapped in southern Ukraine struggle to survive
alerts.summary: Thousands of civilians trapped in frontline communities in southern Ukraine are facing a deepening humanitarian crisis as access to food, medical care and evacuation routes continues to shrink, the UN Human Rights Monitoring Mission in the country, HMRRU, warned on Wednesday.

alerts.title: Stranded Hormuz seafarers begin mass evacuation operation
alerts.summary: As the UN International Maritime Organization (IMO) released more details of its plan to evacuate more than 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, one mariner caught up in the emergency has described the ever-present fear of coming under attack.

alerts.title: World News in Brief: Students injured in Sudan drone strike, dangerous returns in south Lebanon, celebrating women diplomats
alerts.summary: Ongoing fighting in the Sudanese city of El Obeid continues to endanger civilians and damage infrastructure, the United Nations said on Wednesday.

alerts.title: Young peacebuilders: Funding matters, but so do trust and partnerships
alerts.summary: Young people working on promoting peace in some of the world’s most fragile and polarized places say financial support remains essential, but money alone will not sustain their efforts to organize youth to participate in peacebuilding.

alerts.title: Security Council LIVE: Open debate on children and armed conflict
alerts.summary: The UN Security Council held on open debate on Thursday focused on strengthening protections for children caught in armed conflict. The Secretary-General's latest report reveals that in 2025, soldiers and Government forces were responsible for more grave violations against children in armed conflict than non-State armed groups, marking a first in 30 years of UN monitoring. &nbsp;The report verified 38,558 grave violations such as killing, recruitment and abduction, affecting 24,174&nbsp;children, many of whom suffered&nbsp;multiple&nbsp;violations.

alerts.title: Israel continues to commit genocide, atrocity crimes by deliberately targeting Palestinian children, UN independent commission finds
alerts.summary: Israeli authorities and security forces have deliberately targeted Palestinian children, resulting in genocide and atrocity crimes in the Gaza Strip and war crimes in the West Bank, a UN independent commission of inquiry said in its latest report released on Tuesday.

alerts.title: World News in Brief: UN launches Hormuz evacuation plan, UNICEF youth champion killed in Gaza, Lebanon ceasefire ‘largely holding’
alerts.summary: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) will begin implementing an evacuation plan for more than 11,000 seafarers stranded in the Strait of Hormuz, the UN agency announced on Tuesday.

alerts.title: Central African Republic peace gains must be preserved: UN envoy
alerts.summary: The Central African Republic (CAR) “has made remarkable and tangible progress towards lasting peace and security” in recent years, but security remains fragile in border areas, including with war-torn Sudan, UN Special Representative Valentine Rugwabiza told the Security Council on Tuesday.&nbsp;

alerts.title: New Security Council resolution upholds accountability for attacks on peacekeepers
alerts.summary: The United Nations Security Council unanimously adopted a resolution on Tuesday that calls for accountability for crimes committed against UN peacekeepers.

alerts.title: ‘Better future for Syria remains within reach,’ UN Security Council hears
alerts.summary: Syria has emerged after more than a decade of crisis with “clear signs of progress”, but acute needs remain and efforts must boost support so the country can “move from survival towards recovery”, UN officials told the Security Council on Monday.

alerts.title: World News in Brief: Pope Leo urges action on hunger, humanitarian strain deepens in Gaza, families return to Lebanon
alerts.summary: Pope Leo called on the international community to renew its commitment to tackling hunger and malnutrition, describing access to adequate food as a “fundamental human right” during a visit to the World Food Programme (WFP) headquarters in Rome on Monday.

alerts.title: UN welcomes fresh Lebanon ceasefire reports as rights experts urge Iran accountability
alerts.summary: The United Nations has welcomed reports of a new ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hezbollah on Friday while warning that civilians on the ground are still fleeing amid ongoing insecurity.

alerts.title: Gaza: Sneeze and you might get shot, warns UNICEF in alert on child killings
alerts.summary: After another deadly night of clashes in Lebanon, aid agencies issued a new alert for Gaza, where 265 Palestinian children have been killed since a ceasefire was announced in October 2025.

alerts.title: UN upholds freedom of movement for peacekeepers in Lebanon
alerts.summary: The United Nations has again called for freedom of movement for its peacekeepers in Lebanon who continue to closely monitor developments in the south of the country, including in the wake of the recent provisional agreement signed by the United States and Iran.

alerts.title: Security Council LIVE: Gaza in the spotlight as dire conditions continue
alerts.summary: The Security Council debated conditions in Gaza at the request of its 10 elected members amid concern that the territory's humanitarian crisis is being overshadowed by wider regional developments. The meeting took place under a ceasefire that has existed in name since October 2025 – but nearly 1,000 Palestinians have been killed, and most Gazans remain displaced. Relief chief Tom Fletcher told ambassadors that “fragile gains” since the truce are “the bare minimum of what Palestinians need.” Full coverage here, and wider Meetings Coverage can be found here.

alerts.title: US-Iran deal: technical work can begin, says atomic energy agency
alerts.summary: The head of the UN-affiliated atomic energy agency on Thursday welcomed the signing of an initial Iran-US memorandum aimed at ending the war, before proposing “to sit down” with both parties to assist with concrete measures such as verification of Iran’s nuclear programme, a key sticking point.

alerts.title: Lebanon: 12 children killed, maimed daily, despite Hezbollah-Israel truce
alerts.summary: More than 100 days of war reveals a shattering snapshot of life for civilians in Lebanon amid Israeli strikes and forced displacement, the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) reported on Wednesday.

alerts.title: UN officials call for urgent action in Yemen to push peace, reduce hunger
alerts.summary: Top UN officials called for bolstered efforts to stave off rising hunger and foster a path towards permanent peace in Yemen as they briefed the Security Council Tuesday morning.

alerts.title: While world waits for details on Iran-US accord, UN calls for Hormuz aid corridor
alerts.summary: As representatives of Iran and the United States reportedly prepared to sign a new peace agreement at the end of the week, the UN on Monday stressed the urgent need to open an aid corridor to transit the choked-off Strait of Hormuz and prevent a global hunger crisis.

alerts.title: Guterres welcomes US-Iran peace deal as ‘critical step’ toward ending conflict
alerts.summary: UN Secretary General António Guterres welcomed on Sunday a new peace deal between the United States and Iran, calling it a “critical step” toward ending the conflict.

alerts.title: Instability, war and closed borders: How aid workers get emergency food to hungry Afghan children
alerts.summary: The odyssey begins in Indonesia and ends, 15,000 kilometres later, in Kabul, the capital of Afghanistan. Along the way, critical food aid destined for young Afghan students crosses nine countries, over land and sea, skirting geopolitical unrest and conflict zones.

alerts.title: Women and girls caught up in Yemen’s ‘forgotten crisis’ bear the heaviest toll as funding falls
alerts.summary: Yemen remains gripped by one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises, with 22 million – out of a population of 35 million – requiring assistance. Women and girls account for half of those in need, and two-thirds of them are of childbearing age, placing reproductive health at the heart of the emergency.

alerts.title: More strikes impact Lebanon hospitals as humanitarian situation deteriorates
alerts.summary: The humanitarian situation in Lebanon is being pushed further into crisis as hostilities continue between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants – and essential services and healthcare are targeted.

alerts.title: UN ‘encouraged’ by talk of possible US-Iran ceasefire deal
alerts.summary: The United Nations continues to monitor developments amid conflicting reports on Friday of a possible ceasefire deal between the United States and Iran, the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General said in New York.

alerts.title: ‘Grave concern’ after dozens of women arrested in Afghanistan for dress violations
alerts.summary: Gender equality agency&nbsp;UN Women is “gravely concerned” by the arrest of at least 30 women in Herat city last weekend in Afghanistan for allegedly violating dress requirements imposed by Taliban authorities.&nbsp;&nbsp;

alerts.title: Three seafarers killed in Hormuz strike as UN warns of widening fallout
alerts.summary: Three Indian seafarers were killed in an attack on an oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, as renewed hostilities in one of the world’s most critical shipping corridors once again heightened concern over food security, fuel prices and broken global supply chains.

alerts.title: Middle East LIVE: Diplomacy in focus as escalation ‘reverberates across borders and continents’, warns UN chief
alerts.summary: Nearly four months after the latest Middle East crisis erupted and despite a fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran, tensions continue to reverberate across the region and beyond. The UN Security Council held a high-level debate on advancing political solutions in the Middle East on Wednesday, amid continuing conflicts, humanitarian emergencies and concerns over regional stability. Follow live in-depth meetings coverage here.

alerts.title: Fresh strikes on Tyre kill eight, as UN puts Lebanon destruction bill at $365 million, and rising
alerts.summary: Lebanon has suffered more than $365 million in damage to buildings across Beirut and Mount Lebanon since the latest escalation, according to a new UN-led assessment released on Tuesday, as fresh strikes in Tyre underscored the fragility of a ceasefire that has failed to stop the fighting.

alerts.title: Palestinians face systematic abuse by Israeli settlers and Hamas alike: Independent investigators
alerts.summary: Palestinian civilians are trapped between escalating settler violence in the occupied West Bank and fear-based Hamas rule in the Gaza Strip, investigators appointed by the UN Human Rights Council said on Tuesday in a new report.
